exercises.title: Size Calculation Verification
exercises.description: Calculate and verify the sizes of ML-KEM components.
tasks: Calculate ek size for each variant using the formula: 12·k·256/8 + 32
Calculate ct size for each variant using: du·k·256/8 + dv·256/8
Verify your calculations match the specification
Calculate total bandwidth for a TLS handshake with each variant
Compare with RSA-2048 and ECDH-P256 key exchange sizes
exercises.expectedOutput: A table with calculated sizes and comparisons to classical algorithms
hints: Remember compression uses du and dv bits per coefficient

exercises.title: Parameter Selection Case Studies
exercises.description: Recommend ML-KEM parameters for specific use cases.
tasks: Recommend parameters for a consumer IoT doorbell camera
Recommend parameters for a bank's internal transaction system
Recommend parameters for a government classified network
Recommend parameters for a public web server
Justify each recommendation based on security needs and constraints
exercises.expectedOutput: Case study document with justified recommendations
hints: Consider bandwidth, processing power, and data sensitivity
IoT devices may have severe constraints

exercises.title: Security Margin Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze the security margins provided by each ML-KEM variant.
tasks: Research the best known attacks on Module-LWE for each parameter set
Calculate the security margin above the target category
Discuss what happens if lattice attacks improve by 10%
Compare with classical algorithm security margins
Assess long-term security confidence for each variant
exercises.expectedOutput: Security analysis document with attack complexity estimates
hints: Core-SVP methodology is used for security estimates

exercises.title: Compression Tradeoff Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze the role of compression in ML-KEM.
tasks: Explain what du and dv compression parameters do
Calculate ciphertext sizes with and without compression
Discuss the impact of compression on decryption failure probability
Explain why ML-KEM-1024 uses higher du and dv values
Propose alternative compression parameters and analyze tradeoffs
hints: Compression reduces from 12 bits (log2(3329)) to du or dv bits
Higher k needs more precision to avoid failures
